observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Require the concerned officers and employees to settle and/or liquidate their
cash advances and strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the grant,
utilization and liquidation of cash advances;
b. Instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to monitor the grant of cash
advances, enforce strictly the prompt liquidation thereof; and no additional
cash advance be granted if previous cash advance has not been liquidated;
and
c. Send final demand letters to officers and employees previously employed by
the Municipality, otherwise, officials responsible for clearing said individuals
from money accountability be held liable for the unsettled cash advances.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the List of Real Property Tax
Receivable for CY 2020 to the Municipal Accountant; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to record the corresponding RPT and
SET Receivables based from the report or list furnished by the Municipal
Treasurer.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Create committee or assign personnel to conduct annual physical count of
Inventories;
b. Prepare the Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); and
c. After the conduct of annual physical count, require the reconciliation of
records of the GSO and Accounting. Likewise, reconciliation of RPCI with
SC and SLC be undertaken.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the:
a. Property Officer to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs using
the prescribed format and maintain individual property card for all PPEs
owned by the LGU; and
b. Property Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile noted
discrepancies in the inventory count and PPE balances.

We recommend that Management require the accountable/ responsible
officers to:
a. Conduct physical count of the local road networks and submit the Report
on the Physical Count of the Road Network System;
b. Prepare a complete Report on Local Road Networks, maintain local road
inventory, road map and property cards for all roads and its component;
c. Maintain subsidiary ledger cards for roads and road components of every
road network and prepare lapsing schedule showing the computation of
the depreciation of each component of the Road Network System
account;
d. Disclose the total Road Network System account in the Notes to Financial
Statements and make adjusting entry regarding depreciation and
derecognition of the replaced asset following the provision of COA
Circular 2015-008; and
e. Provide a complete description and cost segregation of road components
for road projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Designate Property/Supply Unit staff to be in charge of monitoring
procured DRRM items;
b. Require Property/Supply Unit staff to prepare Inventory List of all
DRRM items, including PPEs and Supplies; and
c. Submit monthly Report of Supplies and Material Issued (RSMI) to
Accounting unit for recording in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Treasurer and other persons responsible to submit the disbursement vouchers
together with complete supporting documents in the total amount of ₱1.72 million to
the Office of the Municipal Accountant for recording and subsequent submission to
the Auditor.

Engineering Office to reinstall the 32 CCTV cameras and make it operational with a
view to ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of
government resources.

        employed by the Municipality, otherwise, officials responsible  for
        clearing said individuals from money accountability be held  liable
        for the unsettled cash advances; and


     d. Request for authority to write-off the unliquidated  cash  advances
        especially those pertaining to deceased accountable officers.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

       a.   Strictly adhere to the regulation that only authorized personnel
          shall be granted with cash advances for proper accountability  and
          responsibility of government funds and properties; and


       b.    Direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  any  other   concerned
          department  heads  to  ensure  that   supporting   documents   are
          sufficient  before  cash  advances  are  granted  to   appropriate
          accountable officers.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the:


     a. Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all properties
        of  the  Municipal  Government   and   prepare   and   submit   the
        corresponding reports to the Audit Team as mandated  by  the  above
        provision; and


     b. Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to maintain
        the prescribed PPELCs and PCs, respectively and reconcile them with
        the subsidiary ledgers and Report on the Physical Count of the  PPE
        (RPCPPE).

We recommended and Management agreed to:

   a) Require the Municipal Accountant to take-up in the books  all  parcels
      of lots which were  officially  received  by  the  LGU  as  government
      property at acquisition cost or  fair  market  value  in  pursuant  to
      Sections 26 and 27 of PPSAS 17 and Section 63 of PD 1445;


   b) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer as the Property Custodian to maintain
      updated report of the LGU’s properties; and


   c) Require the Municipal Assessor’s Office to facilitate the registration
      of the untitled land and/or transfer of title  of  the  aforementioned
      parcels of lots in the name of the LGU with the Registry of Deeds.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the  Municipal  Accountant
to:

      a) Prepare  the  journal  entry  voucher  (JEV)  for  the  unadjusted
         reconciling items of reciprocal accounts and update  recording  of
         transactions at all times; and


      b) conduct periodic reconciliation  of  the  reciprocal  accounts  in
         order to identify possible errors/adjustments  which  need  to  be
         recorded in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed to:


     a. direct the Municipal Accountant and  Treasurer  and  other  persons
        responsible in completing the supporting documents  to  submit  the
        soonest time to the Auditor’s Office the  unsubmitted  disbursement
        vouchers with complete supporting documents and  the  corresponding
        journal entry vouchers;


     b. require all claimants to receive checks only from the Office of the
        Municipal Treasurer to avoid DVs  and  their  supporting  documents
        getting lost/misplaced and for prompt  submission  thereof  to  the
        Office of the Municipal Accountant; and


     c. instruct the Municipal Accountant  and  Treasurer  to  ensure  that
        subsequent  required  reports  and   accounts/DVs   with   complete
        supporting documents are submitted to  COA  within  the  prescribed
        period.

We  recommended  and  Management  agreed  to  direct   the   Municipal
Accountant and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the  above-
cited provisions otherwise cause the suspension  of  the  payment  of  their
salaries until  such  time  that  the  requirements  of  the  submission  of
accounts are duly complied with.

limited government funds, be guided by sound judgment and by existing  rules
and  regulations  and  refrain  from  incurring  unnecessary  and  excessive
expenditures.  We likewise recommend that Management submit  the  additional
documents mentioned above for further evaluation and audit to forestall  the
issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.
